description: A method for spatial alignment of single cell transcriptomic data. See documentation for details on use cases.
author: Julia Kononova, Omar Halawa - GenePattern;GenePattern @ Mesirov Lab, UCSD
plan: ## Tangram GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan ### 1. Overview Tangram is a GenePattern module that A method for spatial alignment of single cell transcriptomic data. See documentation for details on use cases. ### 2. Parameters The module exposes 28 parameters: - Required: single.cell.data.file, cell.type.metadata.field, spatial.data.file, classification.mode, cross.val.mode, training.mode, num.top.DE.genes, alignment.mode, alignment.cell.density, num.epochs, spot.size, scale.factor, spatial.opacity, training.opacity, testing.opacity, deconv.opacity, deconv.point.size, training.bin.num, testing.bin.num, color.mapping.perc, debug, verbose, output.file.tag, genes.to.plot.data.file - Optional: cluster.metadata.field, histological.image.file, img.metadata.field, training.genes.input ### 3. Command Line ``` python /Tangram/main.py --device "cpu" ``` ### 4. Docker Image Docker image: omarhalawa/tangram\:v1.0 ### 5. Validation Strategy - Validate all required parameters are provided - Validate file formats match expected extensions - Check parameter value ranges where applicable ### 6. Implementation Roadmap Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation Phase 2: Docker container setup Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: single.cell.data.file
parameters.description:
Single-cell gene expression data file.
Takes a .h5ad format file (see here).

parameters.name: cell.type.metadata.field
parameters.description: Field name in the scRNA/snRNA-Seq data containing cell type groupings.

parameters.name: spatial.data.file
parameters.description:
Spatial gene expression data file.
Takes a .h5ad format file (see here).

parameters.name: cluster.metadata.field
parameters.description: Field name in the spatial data containing cluster groupings. This parameter is optional.

parameters.name: histological.image.file
parameters.description: A .zip file of a zarr store containing a histology image (only provide input to run the optional deconvolution step).

parameters.name: img.metadata.field
parameters.description: Name of the image layer in the histological image data to process during deconvolution.

parameters.name: classification.mode
parameters.description: Mode of classification to carry out Tangram as either one of "Test-Train" or "Cross-Validation."

parameters.name: cross.val.mode
parameters.description: If "Cross-Validation" is selected, specifies the mode of cross-validation to carry ("Leave-One-Out" or "10-Fold").

parameters.name: training.mode
parameters.description:
Mode of training for Tangram algorithm, does one of the following\:
  1. "Top N Genes"; uses top N differentially expressed genes stratified across cell types (set N in next parameter)
  2. "GMT File Input"; input a .gmt file where the first line's genes are used to train the model (training genes input)
  3. "All Genes"; all genes shared between the two datasets
Default is "Top N Genes."

parameters.name: num.top.DE.genes
parameters.description:
Number of top differentially expressed genes, stratified across cell types and shared between the two datasets, to use for training the prediction model.
\nOnly considered if training mode is set to "Top N Genes."
\nDefault is 100.

parameters.name: training.genes.input
parameters.description:
File input for the genes to use in model training.
Reads a single-line .gmt file.
Only considered if training mode is set to "GMT File Input."

parameters.name: alignment.mode
parameters.description:
Mode of alignment/mapping of either\:
  1. "cells"; mapping at single-cell resolution
  2. "clusters"; averages single cells of the same cluster via the cell type metadata field annotation
  3. "constrained"; for deconvolution, adds a filter term to the loss function
Default is "cells."

parameters.name: alignment.cell.density
parameters.description:
Alignment cell density. Use "rna count-based" if cell density is proportional to number of RNA molecules and "uniform" if spatial voxels are at single cell resolution.
\n\nDefault is RNA count-based.

parameters.name: num.epochs
parameters.description:
Integer value for number of epochs, or iterations through the entire training dataset for the mapping process.
Default is 1000.

parameters.name: spot.size
parameters.description:
Diameter of gene plot spots.
Only considered if no 'spatial' field in spatial input.
Default is 50.

parameters.name: scale.factor
parameters.description:
Gene plot scaling factor to map dots into space.
Only considered if no 'spatial' field in spatial input.
Default is 0.1.

parameters.name: spatial.opacity
parameters.description:
Opacity value for single-cell scanpy spatial plot of original data.
Default is 0.7.

parameters.name: training.opacity
parameters.description:
Opacity value for training score plots.
Default is 0.5.

parameters.name: testing.opacity
parameters.description:
Opacity value for testing score plots.
Default is 0.5.

parameters.name: deconv.opacity
parameters.description:
Opacity value for single-cell scanpy spatial plot of post-deconvolution cell plot.
Default is 0.2.

parameters.name: deconv.point.size
parameters.description:
Point size for single-cell scanpy spatial plot of post-deconvolution cell plot.
Default is 0.4.

parameters.name: training.bin.num
parameters.description:
Number of bins in the training plot histogram.
Default is 20.

parameters.name: testing.bin.num
parameters.description:
Number of bins in the testing plot histogram.
Default is 20.

parameters.name: color.mapping.perc
parameters.description:
Percentile float value for setting color map range (a smaller value corresponds to the color map having a greater coverage of values).
Default is 0.02.

parameters.name: debug
parameters.description: Debugging parameter for developers.

parameters.name: verbose
parameters.description: Verbosity parameter.

parameters.name: output.file.tag
parameters.description:
Output filename tag to be used as a suffix for this run's output.
It is encouraged to change this parameter for every run you intend to do as to differentiate between outputs.
\nDefault uses the basenames of both the scRNA/snRNA-seq and spatial input datasets.

parameters.name: genes.to.plot.data.file
parameters.description: Input data file for genes to compare measured vs predicted data of. Reads the initial line of a .gmt file.
